content.text: The system shall display a Navigation Toolbar within the Digital Twin Rack Layout Builder to provide users with model navigation and status information.
a Navigation Toolbar within the Digital Twin Rack Layout Builder
I can access model navigation controls and view selection status information.
the user is in the Digital Twin Rack Layout Builder
the system displays a Navigation Toolbar containing navigation controls and selection status information
The system shall display a Navigate control that allows users to activate navigation mode for interacting with the 3D layout model.
I can activate navigation mode for interacting with the 3D model.
the system displays a Navigate control
the user can activate Navigation Mode
The system shall visually indicate when Navigation Mode is active.
a visual indication when Navigation Mode is active
I know I am in navigation mode.
the system visually highlights or indicates the active Navigation Mode state
The system shall display the current count of selected Nodes (N) in the toolbar.
see the number of selected nodes
I can track my current selection.
the system displays the current selected Node count (N)
The system shall automatically update the Node count (N) whenever nodes are selected or deselected in the layout.
the toolbar always reflects my current selection.
nodes can be selected or deselected in the layout
the system automatically updates the Node count (N).
The system shall display the current count of selected Lines (L) in the toolbar.
see the number of selected lines
I can track my current selection.
the system displays the current selected Line count (L)
The system shall automatically update the Line count (L) whenever lines are selected or deselected in the layout.
the toolbar always reflects my current selection.
lines can be selected or deselected in the layout
the system automatically updates the Line count (L)
The system shall display the current count of selected Plates (P) in the toolbar.
see the number of selected plates
I can track my current selection.
the system displays the current selected Plate count (P)
The system shall automatically update the Plate count (P) whenever plates are selected or deselected in the layout.
the toolbar always reflects my current selection.
plates can be selected or deselected in the layout
the system automatically updates the Plate count (P)
The system shall display the counts for Nodes (N), Lines (L), and Plates (P) simultaneously to provide users with real-time selection information.
Node, Line, and Plate counts displayed together
I can view complete selection information at a glance.
the system displays Node (N), Line (L), and Plate (P) counts simultaneously
the counts reflect the current selection state in real time
The system shall provide a Clear Selection control that allows users to remove all current element selections from the layout.
I can quickly remove all active selections from the layout.
one or more nodes, lines, or plates are selected
the user views the Navigation Toolbar
the system displays a Clear Selection control
Upon clicking the Clear Selection control, the system shall deselect all selected nodes, lines, and plates and reset their displayed counts to zero.
all selections cleared and counts reset
I can start a new selection without manually deselecting elements.
one or more nodes, lines, or plates are selected
the system deselects all selected nodes, lines, and plates
the system resets the displayed Node (N), Line (L), and Plate (P) counts to zero
The system shall display model statistics including the total number of Nodes, Lines, and Plates present in the current layout.
I can understand the size and composition of the layout.
the system displays the total number of Nodes, Lines, and Plates in the current layout
The system shall automatically refresh the displayed model statistics whenever elements are added, deleted, copied, mirrored, or modified.
model statistics updated automatically when the layout changes
elements are added, deleted, copied, mirrored, or modified
the system automatically refreshes the displayed Nodes, Lines, and Plates totals
The system shall display a Save Status Indicator showing the current persistence state of the layout.
see the current save status of the layout
I know whether my changes have been persisted.
the system displays a Save Status Indicator
When all changes have been successfully saved, the system shall display a Saved status indicator.
a Saved status displayed when all changes are persisted
I know my work has been successfully saved.
all layout changes have been successfully saved
the system displays a "Saved" status indicator
The system shall visually distinguish the Saved status using a status icon, color indicator, or status label.
the Saved status to be visually identifiable
I can quickly recognize the current save state.
the layout is in a saved state
the Save Status Indicator is displayed
the system visually distinguishes the Saved status using a status icon, color indicator, status label, or equivalent visual cue
If there are unsaved changes in the layout, the system shall update the save status indicator to reflect the current save state.
the save status to reflect unsaved changes
I know when the layout contains modifications that have not been saved.
the system updates the Save Status Indicator to reflect the current unsaved state

19.title: Update Toolbar Information in Real Time

content.text: The system shall update all toolbar counts and status indicators in real time without requiring the user to refresh the page.
I always see the latest model and selection status.
toolbar counts or status indicators are displayed
selections, model data, or save states change
the system updates all toolbar counts and status indicators in real time
the system does not require a page refresh
The system shall ensure that toolbar information accurately reflects the current state of the active Digital Twin layout model.
toolbar information to accurately represent the active model
I can rely on the displayed data while working.
the user is working in an active Digital Twin layout model
model data, selections, or save states change
the system ensures all toolbar counts, statistics, and status indicators accurately reflect the current state of the active layout mode
The system shall automatically initiate a synchronization process when a user opens a project in the Digital Twin Rack Layout Builder.
the project to synchronize automatically when I open it
I always work with the latest available project data.
a user opens a project in the Digital Twin Rack Layout Builder
the system automatically initiates a synchronization process
The system shall automatically initiate a synchronization process when the user refreshes the browser page while working on a project.
the project to synchronize automatically after refreshing the page
I receive the latest project updates.
a project is open in the Digital Twin Rack Layout Builder
the user refreshes the browser page
the system automatically initiates a synchronization process.
During synchronization, the system shall display a Sync Status Indicator in the status bar to inform the user that project data is being synchronized.
I know when project data is being updated.
the system displays a Sync Status Indicator in the status bar
The system shall display the synchronization progress percentage (e.g., "Syncing 38%") while the synchronization process is in progress.
I know how much of the process has completed.
the system displays the synchronization percentage (e.g., "Syncing 38%").
The system shall continuously update the displayed synchronization percentage as synchronization progresses.
I can monitor progress in real time.
the system continuously updates the displayed synchronization percentage.
The system shall synchronize the latest project geometry, element properties, grid data, assignments, and other project-related configurations from the server.
all project-related data synchronized from the server
my project remains up to date.
project data is retrieved from the server
the system synchronizes the latest geometry, element properties, grid data, assignments, and other project configurations.
Upon successful completion of synchronization, the system shall remove the Syncing progress indicator and display the current project state as fully synchronized.
a clear indication when synchronization completes successfully
I know the project is fully updated.
the system removes the Syncing progress indicator
the system displays the project as fully synchronized
The system shall ensure that the project displayed to the user reflects the most recently saved version after synchronization is completed.
the latest saved project version displayed after synchronization
I can work with the most current data.
the system displays the most recently saved version of the project
If synchronization fails or is interrupted, the system shall notify the user that the project data could not be fully synchronized.
I am aware that the project may not be fully updated.
the system displays a notification indicating that project data could not be fully synchronized

30.title: Update Synchronization Status in Real Time

content.text: The system shall update the synchronization status indicator in real time until the synchronization process is completed, cancelled, or fails.
synchronization status updates throughout the process
I can track the current state of synchronization.
the system updates the synchronization status indicator in real time
Until the synchronization is completed, cancelled, or fails.
The system shall perform synchronization automatically in the background whenever a project is opened or refreshed, without requiring the user to manually trigger the process.
synchronization to occur automatically in the background
I always work with the latest project data without manual intervention.
a project is opened or refreshed
the system automatically performs synchronization in the background
the system does not require the user to manually trigger synchronization.
The system shall maintain the visibility of the synchronization status indicator until synchronization reaches completion or an error condition occurs.
the synchronization status indicator to remain visible during synchronization
I can monitor the synchronization process.
the system keeps the synchronization status indicator visible
Until synchronization completes successfully or an error occurs.

33.title: Differentiate Sync Status from Save Status

content.text: The system shall display the synchronization status independently of the Save Status Indicator so that users can distinguish between project synchronization and project save operations.
synchronization status displayed separately from save status
I can distinguish between synchronization and save operations.
the system displays the Synchronization Status Indicator independently of the Save Status Indicator.
The system shall display a Merge Lines option in the context menu
a Merge Lines option available in the context menu
I can combine eligible split line segments.
the system displays a Merge Lines option.

35.title: Enable Merge Lines for Eligible Selections

content.text: The system shall enable the Merge Lines option only when  one or more eligible line segments of the same continuous line, separated by an intermediate node are selected in the Digital Twin Rack Layout Builder.
the Merge Lines option enabled only for valid line selections
I can perform merge operations only when supported.
one or more line segments are selected
the selected lines form the same continuous line separated by an intermediate node
the system enables the Merge Lines option.
The system shall disable or prevent execution of the Merge Lines option when the selected lines do not meet the criteria required for merging.
unsupported line selections cannot be merged.
selected lines do not meet merge criteria
the user attempts to use Merge Lines
the system disables the Merge Lines option or prevents its execution.
Upon selecting Merge Lines, the system shall merge the eligible line segments into a single continuous line element.
eligible line segments merged into a single line
the system merges the selected line segments into a single continuous line element.
During the merge operation, the system shall remove the intermediate split between the selected line segments and restore the original continuous line geometry.
intermediate line splits removed during merging
the original continuous line geometry is restored.
the system creates the merged line
the system removes the intermediate split between the selected line segments
The system shall retain the start node and end node of the resulting merged line after the merge operation is completed.
the resulting merged line to retain its end points
the system retains the start node and end node of the resulting line.
If the intermediate node exists solely for the purpose of splitting the line and is not referenced by any other geometry, the system shall remove the intermediate node as part of the merge operation.
an intermediate node exists solely because the line was split
the node is not referenced by any other geometry
the system removes the intermediate node.
The system shall preserve the geometric alignment and position of the resulting merged line after the merge operation.
merged lines to maintain their original alignment
the system preserves the geometric alignment and position of the resulting line.
The system shall preserve all applicable line properties, attributes, and metadata associated with the original line when creating the merged line.
important configuration data is not lost.
line segments contain properties, attributes, or metadata
the system preserves all applicable properties, attributes, and metadata in the merged line.
Upon successful completion of the merge operation, the system shall refresh the rack layout view and display the merged line in the model.
the layout view refreshed after merging
I can immediately see the result.
the system refreshes the rack layout view
displays the merged line in the model.
Upon successful merge the system shall display a success toast “Merged 2 lines -> 1 line, remove 1 node”
confirmation when a merge operation succeeds
I know the action completed successfully.
the system displays the toast message:
The system shall update the element counts and model statistics to reflect the reduced number of line elements after a successful merge.
the system updates element counts and model statistics to reflect the reduced number of line elements.
